name: Czechia

colors.symbolism: from the silver lion of the historic arms of Bohemia, on a red field
from the red field of the historic arms of Bohemia
the heraldic colours of Moravia and Slovakia, and the third pan-Slavic colour

description: A field divided per pall (three regions meeting at the centre of the flag): a white triangle above, a red triangle below, and a blue isosceles triangle along the hoist whose apex meets the horizontal meridian at the centre.
history: Designed by Jaroslav Kursa and adopted by Czechoslovakia on 30 March 1920, replacing the plain white-over-red bicolour of Bohemia (which proved indistinguishable from the Polish flag). The blue hoist triangle was added to incorporate the colours of Moravia and Slovakia and to distinguish the design internationally. After the dissolution of Czechoslovakia on 31 December 1992, the Czech Republic retained the Czechoslovak flag — in apparent contravention of Article 3 of the Czech-Slovak constitutional law on dissolution which forbade either successor from using the federal symbols — and the choice was confirmed by Article 14 of Constitutional Act No. 3/1993 Coll. Slovakia adopted a new flag of its own.

features.symbolism: the field is divided per pall into three triangles — white upper and red lower from the historic Bohemian arms, with the blue hoist triangle added in 1920 to incorporate the colours of Moravia and Slovakia and distinguish the Czechoslovak flag from the very similar Polish bicolour
